title: Exostema dissimiliflorum
intro: Exostema dissimiliflorum, commonly known as "Santa Maria" or "Exostema", is a plant species native to the tropical regions of Central and South America, belonging to the family Rubiaceae. The plant has been traditionally used in folk medicine to treat various health issues, including fever, rheumatism, and digestive problems, due to its anti-inflammatory, antimicrobial, and antioxidant properties. The bioactive constituents of Exostema dissimiliflorum include flavonoids, phenolic acids, and iridoids, which are responsible for its medicinal properties. In traditional herbal medicine, Exostema is often prepared as a decoction, tea, or tincture, and is used to alleviate symptoms of colds, flu, and other respiratory infections.
